“PLEASE, JUST COME OVER RIGHT NOW”

Matthew had never been the type to care about school. He never understood why people stressed over grades, why they spent hours studying instead of actually living. But tonight, as he lay in his dorm, staring at his phone, waiting for a reply that wasn’t coming, he finally understood the feeling the frustration of being second place to something you didn’t even think mattered. For once, Matthew wasn’t losing to another person. He was losing to textbooks and notes, to exams and deadlines. And even though he wouldn’t admit it, it didn’t sit right with him.
